controller.title: Connect to dae External Controller
controller.hint_meta: Enter url and token to connect.
controller.hint: Connect to the dae external controller to start syncing Dashboard, Traffic, Proxies, Logs and config.dae in realtime.
controller.log_level_note: Updates runtime log level via PATCH /configs and keeps dashboard in sync.
controller.embedded_hint: Dashboard is hosted on a dae controller. Just enter the token.
controller.embedded_direct: Accessing current controller. Enter token to establish connection.
controller.login_failed: Login failed, please check if the token is correct.
controller.connection_failed: Cannot connect to controller, please ensure dae is running.
controller.connecting_channel: Connecting to controller and establishing real-time channel.

proxies.waiting: Connect to a controller to load groups and nodes.
proxies.empty: No proxies found. Connect to a controller to load groups and nodes.
proxies.delay: current: {now} · {count} node(s) · Use top-right button to test latency

config.editor_note: Edit config.dae with collapsable sections; use GUI Builder for common blocks, fallback to Raw text for complex syntax.
config.section_include: Split configuration entry and include paths.
config.section_global: Global runtime parameters, controller, network interfaces and dialing behavior.
config.section_dns: DNS upstreams, bind addresses and DNS routing.
config.section_group: Proxy groups, routing policies and node filtering.
config.section_document: Edit the entire document when un-splittable.
config.section_default: Edit the raw text of this top-level dae block.
config.advanced_note: Unstructured content detected, preserved on save; switch to Raw to edit directly.
config.global_add_note: Global key-values not in the form above are appended as key: value.
config.group_add_note: Add group name here, and fill in policy, filter, etc.
config.group_cards_note: Each group has a separate card to easily add and organize policy blocks.
config.routing_rules_note: Add rules in sequence, delete on the right, and maintain fallback separately at the end.

logs.unsupported: Current controller does not support /logs stream.
logs.status_waiting_controller: Connect to remote controller to start log stream.
logs.status_paused: Log stream paused locally, will resume receiving new events upon unpausing.
